---
title: "Unit 7: Assumptions of models"
subtitle: "Data wrangling"
html-math-method:
  method: mathjax
  url: "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-mml-chtml.js"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    self-contained: true
    chalkboard: false
    standalone: true
    slide-number: true
    show-slide-number: all
code-annotations: hover
bibliography: QMLS_Bibliography.bib
csl: evolution.csl
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    echo: true
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false

library(tidyverse)

theme_set(theme_classic(base_size = 20))

ssPlot <- function(
    X,
    Y,
    b,
    do.plot = TRUE,
    do.labels = TRUE,
    xlab = NULL,
    ylab = NULL
) {
    require(latex2exp)
    n <- length(X)
    pred <- (X * b + (mean(Y) - b * mean(X)))
    SSy <- sum((Y - pred)^2)

    M <- tibble(X, Y, pred)

    if (is.null(xlab)) {
        xlab <- "X"
    }
    if (is.null(ylab)) {
        ylab <- "Y"
    }

    if (do.plot) {
        p <- ggplot() +
            geom_point(
                data = tibble(X = mean(X), Y = mean(Y)),
                aes(X, Y),
                color = "navy",
                size = 7,
                pch = 1
            ) +
            geom_abline(
                slope = b,
                intercept = mean(Y) - b * mean(X),
                color = "navy",
                linewidth = 1
            ) +
            geom_segment(
                data = M,
                aes(x = X, xend = X, y = Y, yend = pred),
                color = "firebrick",
                linewidth = 1
            ) +
            geom_point(data = M, aes(x = X, y = Y), size = 3) +
            labs(x = xlab, y = ylab)

        v1 <- round(b, 2)
        v2 <- round(SSy, 2)

        ll1 <- glue::glue("$\\theta_1 = {v1}$")
        ll2 <- glue::glue("$\\SS = {v2}$")

        xpos <- min(X) + 0.15 * diff(range(X))
        ypos <- min(Y) + 0.8 * diff(range(Y))

        if (do.labels) {
            p <- p +
                annotate(
                    geom = "text",
                    label = TeX(ll1, output = "character"),
                    x = xpos,
                    y = ypos,
                    parse = TRUE,
                    hjust = 0,
                    size = 9
                ) +
                annotate(
                    geom = "text",
                    label = TeX(ll2, output = "character"),
                    x = xpos,
                    y = ypos * 0.96,
                    parse = TRUE,
                    hjust = 0,
                    size = 9
                )
        }
    } else {
        p <- NULL
    }
    return(list(SSy, p))
}

```


# Housekeeping {background-color="#40666e"}

- PC 1 Key
- Micropublication Data / topic due Tuesday after Spring Break


# Common issues with data {background-color="#40666e"}


## Common issues with data

- Missing (and shouldn't be)
- Data entry error (decimal place shift)
- Spelling error
- Mis-coding of categorical variables (`f` and `F`, trailing spaces)


## Identifying problematic data

- Unique values and counting
- Plotting
- Making tables, sorting
- `max()` and `min()`, `which.max()` and `which.min()`


## Data to work with: Swallowtail Butterflies

![](http://www.pyrgus.de/bilder1/papilionidae/machaon_im2023.jpg){fig-align="center" fig-alt="Photo of a swallotail butterfly on a plant."}

- 2 Sub-species, 2 sexes
- Forewing and hindwing areas
- Tail length


## Data to work with: Swallowtail Butterflies

```{r}
STB <- read_csv(
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/QM-Life-Sci/RDABR/refs/heads/main/data/Swallowtail.csv",
    show_col_types = FALSE
)
glimpse(STB)

STB |> count(Species, Sex)
```

## Unique values and counts

```{r}
unique(STB$Species)

STB |> count(Species, Sex)
```


## Plotting

```{r}
#| echo: false

STB <- STB |>
    mutate(
        ID = 1:n(),
        Forewing_area = replace(Forewing_area, Forewing_area == 5.523, 55.23)
    ) |>
    relocate(ID)
```

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot of hingwing area versus forewing area. There is a postive relationship with one extreme value with a very large forewing area."

ggplot(STB, aes(Forewing_area, Hindwing_area)) +
    geom_point()
```


## Plotting with IDs labeled

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot of hingwing area versus forewing area. There is a postive relationship with one extreme value with a very large forewing area. Points are labeled with their index number."

ggplot(STB, aes(Forewing_area, Hindwing_area, label = ID)) +
    geom_label()
```


## Sorting: `arrange()`

```{r}
STB |> arrange(desc(Forewing_area)) |> slice(1:5)
```

Sort smallest to largest and print the last rows: `slice_tail()`

```{r}
STB |> arrange(Forewing_area) |> slice_tail(n = 5)
```


## Maximum and minimum

```{r}
max(STB$Forewing_area)
```

Using `which.max()`

```{r}
STB |> slice(which.max(Forewing_area))

STB |> slice_max(Forewing_area)
```

Many `slice_...()` functions.


## Locating errors

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot of hingwing area versus forewing area. There is a postive relationship with one extreme value with a very large forewing area."

STB <- read_csv(
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/QM-Life-Sci/RDABR/refs/heads/main/data/Swallowtail.csv",
    show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
    mutate(
        ID = 1:n(),
        Forewing_area = replace(Forewing_area, Forewing_area == 5.523, 55.23)
    ) |>
    relocate(ID)

ggplot(STB, aes(Forewing_area, Hindwing_area)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkgreen")
```


```{r}
STB |> arrange(desc(Forewing_area)) |> slice(1:5)
STB$Forewing_area[176]
```


## Replacing values: `replace()`

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot of hingwing area versus forewing area. The extreme value has been replaced to be correct. There is a clear positive relationship."

STB_correct <- STB |>
    mutate(
        Forewing_area = replace(Forewing_area, Forewing_area == 55.23, 5.523)
    )

ggplot(STB_correct, aes(Forewing_area, Hindwing_area)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkgreen")
```


# Questions {background-color="#f8c471"}


# Assumptions {background-color="#40666e"}

:::{.center}
Requirements for sound inference
:::


## Assumptions

Every model has *assumptions* that must be met to ensure an unbiased analysis

*Diagnostics* can evaluate models *after they are fit*:

1. Linearity
2. Independence
3. Equal variance
4. (Approximately) normal residuals

2 and 3: "Independent and identically distributed" (IID)


## Residuals

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot of age plotted against proportion black. The line of best fit is plotted. Vertical lines connect the each point to the line, showing the residual value. The mean of x and mean of y is plotted as a circle."

LA <- read_csv(
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Middleton-Lab/abdData/refs/heads/main/inst/extdata/datasets/17/17e1LionAges.csv",
    show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
    rename(
        Age = age,
        `
Proportion
Black
` = proportion.black
    )
fm <- lm(
    Age ~ `
Proportion
Black
`,
    data = LA
)
l <- ssPlot(
    X = LA$`
Proportion
Black
`,
    Y = LA$Age,
    b = coef(fm)[2],
    xlab = "Proportion Black",
    ylab = "Age",
    do.labels = FALSE
)
print(l[[2]] + scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)))
```


## Problematic residuals

![](https://openintro-ims.netlify.app/inf-model-slr_files/figure-html/fig-whatCanGoWrongWithLinearModel-1.png){fig-align="center" width=100% fig-alt="Grid of images showing problematic residual patterns. The set of images on the top are y versus x and the set on the bottom are corresponding residuals versus predicted y."}

From [Introduction to Modern Statistics](https://openintro-ims.netlify.app/) (Çetinkaya-Rundel and Hardin, 2021)


## Model summary `Residuals`

- `Median` should be ~ 0
- `Min` and `Max` should be approximately equal
- `1Q` and `3Q` should be approximately equal

```{r}
fm <- lm(Hindwing_area ~ Forewing_area, data = STB)
summary(fm)
```


## Checking residuals

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot of hingwing area versus forewing area. There is a postive relationship with one extreme value with a very large forewing area. The line of best fit is overlaid."

ggplot(STB, aes(Forewing_area, Hindwing_area)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", se = FALSE)
```


## Extracting residuals

```{r}
fm$residuals[1:10]

library(broom)
STB <- augment(fm, STB)
str(STB)
```


## Bivariate model diagnostics

[Performance package](https://easystats.github.io/performance/articles/check_model.html): `check_model()`

- Posterior predictive checks: `check_predictions()`
- Homogeneity of variance: `check_heteroskedasticity()`
- Normality of residuals: `check_normality()`
- Influential observations: `check_outliers()`

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| output-location: slide
#| fig-alt: "A set of 5 plots displaying the model diagnostics for posterior predictive check, linearity, homogeneity of variance, influential observations, and normality of residuals."

library(performance)
library(see)

check_model(fm)
```


## Posterior predictive check

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "The plot showing the posterior predictive check. The observed density plot for hindwing area is a green line. Several model-predicted lines are overlaid in blue. These largely overlap with the green line."

PP <- plot(check_model(fm, panel = FALSE))
PP[[1]]
```

## Linearity

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Linearity check showing the plot of Residuals versus Fitted values. A dashed horizontal line is at zero. The points are in blue and fall along a curved green line."

PP[[2]]
```


## Homogeneity of variance

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Homogeneity of variance check showing the square root of the standard residuals versus the fitted values. Blue points fall along the green fitted line. These form a v for low values and then a curve."

PP[[3]]
```


## Influential observations

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Influential observations check showing Standard residuals vs Leverage. Two horizontal green lines show where points should fall. The fitted green line shows a sigmoid pattern with most points falling within the lines and one red extreme value."

PP[[4]]
```


## Normality of residuals

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Normality of residuals check showing Quantile deviations versus standard normal quantiles. The green horizontal line at zero is the reference. The blue points cross this line diagonally."

PP[[5]]
```


## Additional checks

```{r}
#| echo: true

check_heteroskedasticity(fm)
check_normality(fm)
check_outliers(fm)
```


## Drop and refit

- `slice()` with `-` drops rows
- `filter()` would also work

```{r}
#| output-location: slide

STB_2 <- STB |> slice(-which.max(Forewing_area))

fm2 <- lm(Hindwing_area ~ Forewing_area, data = STB_2)
summary(fm2)
```


## Model check

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "A set of 5 plots displaying the model diagnostics for posterior predictive check, linearity, homogeneity of variance, influential observations, and normality of residuals."

check_model(fm2)
```


## Posterior predictive check

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "The plot showing the posterior predictive check. The observed density plot for hindwing area is a green line. Several model-predicted lines are overlaid in blue. These largely overlap with the green line."

PP <- plot(check_model(fm2, panel = FALSE))
PP[[1]]
```

## Linearity

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Linearity check showing the plot of Residuals versus Fitted values. A dashed horizontal line is at zero. Blue points are scattered along the green fitted line. The fitted line is roughly horizontal."

PP[[2]]
```


## Homogeneity of variance

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Homogeneity of variance check showing the square root of the standard residuals versus the fitted values. Blue points are scattered along the green fitted line. The fitted line is roughly horizontal."

PP[[3]]
```


## Influential observations

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Influential observations check showing Standard residuals vs Leverage. Two horizontal dashed green lines show where points should fall. The fitted green line shows horizontal line and all points fall within the dashed green lines."

PP[[4]]
```


## Normality of residuals

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Normality of residuals check showing Quantile deviations versus standard normal quantiles. The green horizontal line at zero is the reference. The blue points fall nicely along the line."

PP[[5]]
```


## Assumptions when comparing means

- Observations are independent
- Observations normally distributed *within* groups
    - Not between groups (e.g., bimodal distribution when all observations are combined)
- Within-group variances are (approximately) equal


## Transformations to meet assumptions

- Transformations are mathematical operations
- Most often to address non-normality of residuals
- e.g., `sqrt()`, `log()`, `log10()`, $x^2$, $x^3$

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot of Body Mass versus Height for Male !Kung. There is a positive relationship."

Kung <- read_delim(
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rmcelreath/rethinking/refs/heads/master/data/Howell1.csv",
    delim = ";",
    show_col_types = FALSE
) |>
    filter(male == 1 & height > 100) |>
    rename(mass = weight)

ggplot(Kung, aes(height, mass)) +
    geom_point(color = "darkblue") +
    labs(
        x = "Height (cm)",
        y = "Body Mass (kg)",
        title = "Mass and Height in Male !Kung"
    )
```


## Naive model

```{r}
fm1 <- lm(mass ~ height, data = Kung)
summary(fm1)
```


## Naive model

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot of Body Mass versus Height for Male !Kung. There is a positive relationship. A line of best fit is overlaid."

ggplot(Kung, aes(height, mass)) +
    geom_point(color = "darkblue") +
    geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "hotpink") +
    labs(
        x = "Height (cm)",
        y = "Body Mass (kg)",
        title = "Mass and Height in Male !Kung"
    )
```


## Model check: Linearity

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Linearity check showing the plot of Residuals versus Fitted values. A dashed horizontal line is at zero. Blue points are scattered along the green fitted line. The fitted line is somewhat sigmoidal."

PP <- plot(check_model(fm1, panel = FALSE))
PP[[2]]
```


## Model check: Residuals

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Normality of residuals check showing Quantile deviations versus standard normal quantiles. The green horizontal line at zero is the reference. The blue points fall nicely along the line."

PP[[5]]
```


## Transformation

The problem:

$$ Mass \sim Height ^3 $$

Log-transformation

$$ \log(Mass) \sim 3 \times \log(Height) $$
$$ \log_{10}(Mass) \sim 3 \times \log_{10}(Height) $$


## Log-log plot

```{r}
#| output-location: slide
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-alt: "Scatterplot of log 10 Body Mass versus log 10 Height for Male !Kung. There is a positive relationship. A line of best fit is overlaid."

ggplot(Kung, aes(height, mass)) +
    geom_point(color = "darkblue") +
    geom_smooth(formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "hotpink") +
    scale_x_log10() + # <1>
    scale_y_log10() + # <1>
    labs(
        x = "log10 Height (cm)",
        y = "log10 Body Mass (kg)",
        title = "Mass and Height in Male !Kung"
    )
```

1. Convert x and y axes to log~10~.

Note: no change to `geom_smooth()`.


## Transform and re-fit

```{r}
Kung <- Kung |>
    mutate(log10_mass = log10(mass), log10_height = log10(height))
fm2 <- lm(log10_mass ~ log10_height, data = Kung)
summary(fm2)
```


## Model check: Linearity

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Linearity check showing the plot of Residuals versus Fitted values. A dashed horizontal line is at zero. Blue points are scattered along the green fitted line. The fitted line is somewhat sigmoidal but less so than the previous check."

PP <- plot(check_model(fm2, panel = FALSE))
PP[[2]]
```


## Model check: Residuals

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Normality of residuals check showing Quantile deviations versus standard normal quantiles. The green horizontal line at zero is the reference. The blue points fall nicely along the line."

PP[[5]]
```


# Questions {background-color="#f8c471"}
